Bedford Schl. ; tel. constrn. supt. agnr., posts and tels. dept., Nigeria, May, 1909 ; aptg. tel. engnr., Jan., 1914 ; asst. engnr.-in-charge, an., 1921 ; P.M.G., July, 1928 ; served with Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1914-16 ; dir., posts and tels. to C.E.F., 1915 ; engnr.-in-ch., 1921 ; M.G., 1928 ; P.M.G., Kenya, Uganda and Tanganyika postal serv., 1936.
EDWARD MICHAEL.—B. 1868; M.H.A. for Placentia and St. Mary's, Newfoundland, 1900; min. of finance and cust., 1900; re-elected to Assembly, 1904; deceased.

HILL ARTHUR HEYLIGER.—B. 1876; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, and New Coll., Eastbourne; clk., treasury, Br. Guiana. 1893; 3rd grade comsry. of taxation, 1902; 2nd grade, 1904; sec. excise bd. and supervising comsry., treasury, in rev. branch, 1905; passed exam. in Hindi, 1905; 1st cls. clk., immigr. dept., 1907; seconded as asst. emigrn. agt., Calcutta, Mar., 1913; ag. emigrn. agt. at Calcutta for Br. Guiana, Trinidad, Fiji and Jamaica, Apr. to Aug., 1914, and from Nov., 1915 to Nov., 1916; immigrn. agt.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1929.

HILL ARTHUR HEYLIGER.—B. 1876; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, and New Coll., Eastbourne; clk., treasys., Br. Guiana. 1893; 3rd grade comsry. of taxation, 1902; 2nd grade, 1904; sec. excise bd. and supervising comsry.. treasys., in. rev. branch, 1905; passed exam. in Hindi, 1905; 1st cls. clk., imigrn. dept., 1907; seconded as asst. emigrn. agt., Calcutta, Mar., 1913; ag. emigrn. agt. at Calcutta for Br. Guiana, Trinidad, Fiji and Jamaica, Apr. to Aug., 1914, and from Nov., 1915 to Nov., 1916; immigrn. agt.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1929.

REGINALD COURTENAY, D.D. — Educated at Magdalen Hall, Oxford, where he graduated B.A., 1835, M.A. 1838; appointed archdeacon of Middlesex in Jamaica in 1853; and coadjutor to the Bishop of Jamaica, under the title of Bishop of Kingston, Feb. 1856; a privy councillor in Jamaica, Sept. 1856.

SIR FREDERICK GORDON, K.C.M.G. (1922), C.M.G. (1908), D.S.O. (1918), Chev. Legion d'Honneur (1917).—B. 1869; passed into R. M. Academy, Woolwich, 1886; 2nd Lieut., Royal Engineers 1889; Lieut., 1892; Capt., 1900; Major, 1908; Lieut.-Col., 1916; Brig.-Gen., 1917; Brevet-Col., 1919; retired from Army, 1919; served in Singapore, 1893 to 1896; instructor, R.M. Academy, 1897-1902; asst. dir. of surveys, Gold Coast, 1902; dir., 1904 to 1908; dir. of surveys, S. Nigeria, 1910; surveyor-general, Nigeria, 1914; rejoined Army on outbreak of war; commanded 94th Field Co., R.E. 1914; C.R.E., 8th Dec., 1915; C.R.E. 66th Div., 1916; commanded 197th Infantry Brigade, Mar., 1917; commanded 170th Infantry Brigade, May, 1917 to July, 1918; asst. inspr.-gen. of Training in France, July, 1918; commanded 100th Infantry Brigade, 1919; governor and commander in chief, Gold Coast, 24th Sept., 1919; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Br. Guiana, 1923; resigned, 1929.

IZARD, GODFREY WALLACE. — B. 1891; ed. Uppingham; B.S.A. Co., 1909-14; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1914; enlisted, Aug., 1914; invalided; admstve. offr., Nigeria, Jan., 1915; admstve. serv., Apr., 1930; dep. comsnr., lands, 1930; prin. asst. sec., N. Provs., 1931; sec., N. Provs., May, 1936. IZARD, H. — Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915.

KIRBY, AUSTIN HENRY, B.A. (Cantab).—1879; agric. and science master attached, on half of Imperial dept. of agric., to the Antigua gram. schl., 1903; lecturer in agric. science to elementary schl. teachers and to students of mining coll., Antigua.

VING, HENRY EDWARD, M.B.E. — Ed. at zerhouse, and Handelslehranstalt, Leipzig; er, 9th Lancers, 1899-1902; served in S.A. al and four clasps); ch. clk. to cent. riation bd., O.R.C., Mar., 1902; clk. to ec., O.R.C., Oct., 1902; prov. sec.'s dept., ; lieut., 16th M.R., 1913; seconded for duty nsor, O.F.S., 1914-19; ment. in desps., ; M.B.E., 1918; ch. clk. to prov. sec., 1924; prov. sec., O.F.S. prov. admstrn., June, ; prov. sec., O.F.S., Nov., 1935.

KIRK, SIR JOHN, M.D., G.C.M.G. (1886), K.C.B., K.C.M.G. (1881), C.M.G. (1879).—1832; formerly H.M.'s polit. agt. and consul-gen. at Zanzibar. KIRKPATRICK, HON. ANDREW ALEXANDER. M.L.C., S. Australia, 1891-97 and 1900; ch. sec. and min. of industry, 1905-1909; agt.-gen. London, 1909.

HEDDON, JOHN DONALD, B.A. (Oxon). — B. 879 ; clk., cust., Port Elizabeth, 1903 ; prin. lk., East London, 1915 ; survr., Durban, 1916 ; h. clk., head office, Pretoria, 1918 ; dep. comsnr., ust. and excise, 1926 ; comsnr. cust. and excise, 1930 ; del. for Union govt. at Imp. Just. Confce., 1921 ; cust. adviser to delegn. to Australian govt. (flour shipments), 1921 ; mem., delegn. to Kenya to rept. on trade prospects, 1922 ; rep. of Union govt. at economic conference, Antananarivo, Madagascar, 1923 ; advisory mem., Imp. Confce. 1926 ; del. for Union govt., Imp. Confce., 1930 ; adviser, Union govt., Ottawa Econ. Confce., 1932. HEDLEY, HAROLD THOMAS, Incorporated Acct. — B. 1886 ; audit dept., S.S., Dec., 1921 ; i/c. external audit dept., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1922 ; asst. audr., grade A, Jan., 1923 ; ag. sr. asst. audr., Nov., 1928-Jly., 1929 ; audr., Penang, Aug., 1930 ; snr. asst. audr., S.S. and F.M.S., July, 1933 ; ag. dep. audr., S.S. and F.M.S., Aug., 1934.
KIRWAN, ARTHUR CONINGSBY.—Jun. clk., col. sec.'s off., Falkland Is., 1st Jan., 1907.
KITTERMASTER, H. B.—Asst. dist. comsnr., A.P., 18th Dec., 1908.

ES, CHARLES WILLIAM, F.R.A.S.—Computer, Royal Observatory, Greenwich; junr. asst., Royal Observatory, Good Hope, 1902; 1st asst., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1907; chief asst., ditto, lir. in 1915, 1920, 1924, 1925 and 1929.
KINTORE, RT. HON. EARL OF, G.C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1852; lord in waiting, 1885; capt. of women of the guard, 1886; privy councillor, 1886; gov. S. Australia, 1889-95; a lord in waiting, 1895.

SHERWOOD, ALBERT ARTHUR MANGNALL, B.E. (1926), M.A. (Oxon) (hons.). — B. 1889; St. John's schl., Leatherhead, and University ll, Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, ly, 1913; Nigerian Land Contingent, 1914; td. polit. dept., N. Nigeria, 1915-17; polit. r., Tanganyika admstrn., 8th Oct., 1917; ag. or. comsnr., Feb., 1923 to Jan., 1924; dep. dir., ucn., Sept., 1924; ag. dir., educn., Oct., 1924 Mar., 1925; ditto and mem., exec. and leg. uns., in 1927, 1929, and 1931; Swahili examr.; r., educn., 1931.

KINGSMILL, HON. WALTER.—B. 1864; M.L.A. Pilbarry, W. Australia, in 1897; ditto, 1901; n. for pub. wks., 1901; comsnr. of rlwys., 1901-2; col. sec. and min. of educn., 1902-4; ag. premier on three occasions; col. sec. and min. of educn., Aug., 1905; chrmn. of comtees., legis. coun., 1906.

KIRKPATRICK, IVONE.—Cadet Sarawak civ. serv., Limbang, Aug., 1892; extra offr., Simanggang, Jan., 1893; res. 2nd cls., Mar., 1898, of Upper Rejang, May, 1899; res. 2nd cls., Sadong, b., 1901; Kapit, 1905.

KINS, G. H., C.M.G. (1891)—Clerk, government railways, Victoria, 1861; clerk of bills in the legislative assembly, 1865; secretary to royal commission on the subject of election of members of the legislature, 1868; chairman of committees, 1870; secretary to the royal commission on the parliament buildings, 1877, and assistant of the legislative assembly, 1878; of the legislative assembly, 1882; secretary to Australasian Federation Convention, 1890. For his services was accorded a special vote of thanks by the members of that body; on 1st May, was appointed clerk of the parliaments of India; is an honorary justice of the peace for the colonies of Victoria and South Australia. On the occasion of his twenty-fifth year of service in the legislative Assembly, the members of that body presented him with a magnificent service of plate as a mark of their regard and esteem, and on his retiring from the position of clerk of the legislative Assembly, a special vote of thanks was passed unanimously for the prolonged and singularly valuable services which he had rendered to the country and its members.
LAMPSON P. E. — Ed. Wesleyan mission schl., Anamaboe, G. Coast; asst. master, govt. schl., Accra, 1893.
KIRKWOOD, RICHARD HUGH.—B. 1859; ag. col. sec.'s office, Fiji, 1888-91; 3rd clk., 1891-2; ch. clk. audit dept., 1892-1907; asst. auditor, 1907; ag. col. auditor, 9th Aug., 1909.

SAACS, RT. HON. SIR ISAAC ALFRED, . (1921), G.C.M.G. (1932), K.C.M.G. (1928).— 1855; called to the bar, Victoria, 1880; Q.C., ; M.L.A., Victoria, 1892-1901; solr.-gen., 3; atty.-gen., 1894-9; and again in 1900; mem. Aust. Fed. Convention, 1897-8; and of first leral parlmt.; atty.-gen. in Deakin ministry, 5-6; judge of High Ct. of Australia, Oct., 6; ag. ch. just., 1925 and 1927; ch. just., r., 1930; gov. gen. of the Commonwealth of stralia, Jan., 1931.
WALTER ALBERT, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1883; Carpenters' Company's Coll. and West tech. Inst.; dist. engr., Br. Guiana, 1914; exec. engr., P.W.D., Tanganyika, 1929.

IVEY, LIEUT. COMMANDER. A. V. P., R.D., .N.R. — B. 1894; Royal Navy, 1915; sub. eut., R.N.R., 1917; lieut. commdr., 1927; commdr. (ret. list), 1935; Cameroons expedy. orce, 1915-16; Harwich force destroyers, 1916-17; ommanded Torpedo boats, 1918; commanded I.M. river gunboat "Robin" on West River, hina, 1919; marine offr., Nigeria, 1920; harb. nast., Port Harcourt, 1926; prin. marine offr., 930; ag. dir., marine, Oct., 1934 to May, 1935; lir., marine, 1935.
LEE, JOHN HENRY BURKILL.—B. 1905; ed. Univ. Coll., Leicester; cadet, Hong Kong, civ. serv., Dec., 1928; asst. treas., Apr., 1931; asst.

NELSON, KT. (BACH.). B.A.—B. 1870; ed. at S. Africa Coll., and R. Schl. of Mines, Clausthal, Germany; gold med., S.A. Coll. and of Chem. Met. and Mining Soc. of S.A.; asst. engnr. and consulting engnr. to Transvaal Gold Fields Ltd., and subsidiary coys., 1896-1907; govt. mining engnr., Transvaal, 1st Jan., 1908; ditto, Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chancellor, Univ. of Witwatersrand; ret., 1st Nov., 1926.
P.M.G., 1932. LEE, LIONEL JAMES, A.M.I.M.E.—Ed. R.N.E. Coll., Keyham; asst. engr., marine dept., Nigeria, 1913; prin. engr., 1927; supt. engr., 1933. LEE, HON. SIR W. H., K.C.M.G. (1922), Kt.

KITCHENER, LIEUT.-GEN. FREDERICK WALTER, C.B. (1902).—B. 1858; served in Afghan war, 1878-80; with Dongola expedn., 1896; 4th dist. Medjidie; in S. African war, 1899-1902; mmdg. 3rd (Lahore) divn., Northern command, India, 1902-8; lieut.-gen., 1906; gov. of Bermuda, 9th Nov., 1908.
BACH. (1920).—M.H.A., Tasmania; premier, chief sec., and min. for educn., 1916-22.
BETTER, SIDNEY.—B. 1902; apptd. pet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

BACH. (1908).—Ed. at Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge (B.A. 1876; M.A. 1879; LL.D. 1884); called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1878; admitted to the bar of the P. ct., Cape Col., 1880; examr. in lit. at Cape Univ. in 1881 and 1886-1889; raised to the bench of the sup. ct. and assigned to the high ct. Griqualand as second puisne judge, 1882; ag. judge pres., 1887; judge pres., 1888; ret., 1913.
LEEMBRUGGEN, EDWARD LESLIE.—B. 1893; regisy. clk., Western Pacific high comsn., 1917; corres. clk., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Col., 1920; sec. to res. comsnr., 1922; sec. to govt., 1931.

LIEUT.-COL. SIR HERBERT, K.C.M.G. E., C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1846; lieut. 1866; employed under War Office at Sydney House in 1868 to 1870, and 1 of sunken ships, from submarine nd under G.P.O. in the telegraph m 1870 to 1873, and 1874 to 1876; the campaign of 1873-74 on the priv. sec. to the Earl of Carnarvon, 7, to Feb., 1878; sec. to the col. comtee., 1878; sec. to the roy. comsn. fence of Br. possessions and commerce 1879-82; specially employed to visit it on the defences of Singapore and 1883-4; sec. col. defence comtee., 1885; to Lord Carnarvon, viceroy of Ireland, 5, to Jan., 1886; sec. to roy. comsn. turne centennial exhibn., 1888; priv. ord-lieut. of Ireland, 1892-5; sec. to comsn. for the Paris exhibition, 1900.

KITCHENER, LT.-COL. ADAM.—B. 1858; statistical Eastern sys. of rlys., Cape Town, Feb., 1881; pres examiner, July, 1882; construction bookkpr., ag., 1883; transfd. to Western system, Dec., 1884; aid bookkpr., 1888; asst. acctnt., 1892; acctnt. dep. of agricul., Nov., 1896; ch. acctnt., May, 1898; accntng. offr., May, 1899; served as lieut. with the P.A.O.C. artilly. in Bechuanaland, 1897; prin. paymaster, col. def. force, Apr., 1901, with th rank of major; lieut.-col., Oct., 1902; acctng. offr. in comsnr. of pub. wks. off., July, 1904; chief paymaster and accntng. offr., treasury., July, 1908.

LAIDLAW. DOUGLAS HUNTER, B.A., M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1880; ed. Leys Schl., Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; mech. sc., tripos, 1901; instr., tech. schl., Kuala Lumpur, Mar., 1906; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Perak, Apr., 1909; ag. ex. engnr., Sept., 1913 and Aug., 1914; ex. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Apr., 1915; ag. irrig. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Sept., 1920; ag. dir. pub. works, Trengganu, Sept., 1921; state engnr., Trengganu, Aug., 1923; ch. hydrau. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Nov., 1927.
FRANK G.—B. 1903; ed. Brentwood nd Downing Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls. tripos, 1923; 1st cls. historical tripos, 1924; foundation schl., 1924; apptd. to ter compet. exam., 8th Nov., 1926; temp.
IIB, 1961–62. (Tang. Govt. service.)
LEE, HON. W. H.—M.H.A., Tasmania; premier, chief sec., and min. for educn., Apr., 1916.

KNAGGS, HARRY LESLIE.—B. 1867; clk., San Fernando Hosp., Trinidad, Apr., 1885; 4th clk. col. sec.'s office, Oct., 1885; 3rd clk., July, 1892; 2nd clk., Nov., 1900; 3rd inspr. of immigts., Aug., 1897, to Nov., 1900; ag. asst. col. sec., 1895, 1899, 1900, 1906 and 1907; ag. col. sec., 1st to 7th May, 1907; sec. to civ. ser. comsn. and to rlys. and roads comsn., 1894; ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., and supt. of govt. printing, Apr., 1906; ag. asst. col. sec., Mar. 1908, to Mar., 1909.

IMERE-GUPPY, GARETH EVERARD.— 4; entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, May, seconded for special serv. to water authority erage bd., 1904-1905; ag. inspr. of imigrts. 1910, 1912 to 1913; statistical clk. and vol. secretariat, Trinidad, July, 1915; some tax comtee., esta. comtee., tenders and civ. serv. comtee.; J.P. for the col. bago; dep. sub-intendant crown lands, 1920; sub.-intend., crown lands, Jan., 1925.

H, FRANK, M.C., B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1889; serv., July, 1917; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1921; attd., secretariat, June, 1921; stat. to asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, 1922; asst. sttlmt. offr., Oct., 1923; office dir., med. and sany. services, Apr., 1929; lmt. offr., Jan., 1930; asst. govt. agt., Feb., 1931; do., Hambantota, Apr., ag. prin., 26th Sept., 1930; seconded to Nyasaland admstrn., 2nd Apr., 1931; asst. pvte. sec. to S. of S. for the Colonies, 4th Aug., 1933.

LEE, H. N., O.B.E. (Mily.), 1918.—Ed. Portsmouth Gram. Schl. and King's Coll. London; articles and exams., Inst. of Chart. Accts., 1906-11; col. audit dept. 1913; asst. audr., E.A.P., Jan., 1914; E.A. Expedy. Force, 1914-19 (twice ment. in desps.); senr. asst. audr., Kenya, 1920; ag. audr., Kenya, 1923; temp. duty Somaliland Prot., 1924-26 and in 1928; ag. audr. Kenya, 1926-27; prin. asst. audr., Jan., 1928; audr., Zanzibar Prot., Nov., 1928; audr., Tanganyika Territory, 1933.

LEESE, CAPT. ERNEST BENJAMIN, O.B.E. (1930).—3rd Batt., E. Yorks Regt., res. of offrs.; served in I.Y. in S. African War, 1900-1901 (Queen's medal with 3 clasps); subaltern in G. Coast Regt., July, 1905; capt., Jan., 1908; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to govr. of G. Coast, Nov., 1910 to Feb., 1911; trav. comsnr., Gambia, Nov., 1911; served with Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1915-16; mem., exec. and leg. couns., Nov., 1929; ag. col. sec., Nov., 1930 to Mar., 1931.

LOGGIE. THOMAS GEORGE JOHNSTON, I.S.O. (1917).—B. 1854; ed. Presbyterian Acad., Chatham, New Brunswick; dep. survr. gen., New Brunswick, 1898; lieut.-col. in the active Militia; low dep. min. of lands and mines.

LEES, AUBREY TREVOR OSWALD.—B. 1899; ed. Repton and Royal Mily. Academy, Woolwich; ent. R. Arty., Jan., 1919; served with 2nd R.W. Fus., Ireland, June, 1920; and with Arab and Kurdish Levies, 'Iraq and Kurdistan, June, 1922; attd. air hd. qrs., 'Iraq (air staff intell., spec. serv.) June, 1924; admstrn., Zanzibar, Feb., 1926; pvte. sec. to Br. res. and ag. asst. sec., Nov., 1926; clk. of exec. and leg. couns. and edr. of gazette, Sept., 1927; temp. attd., C.O., 1928; admstve offr., Palestine, 1929.

TITT, CLYDE.—B. 1878; ed., High Schl., Mich., Univ. of Mich. (B.A., 1901; or., 1904); ent. U.S. forest serv. as student 1903; apptd. forest asst., 1904, with suc- ppts. as ch. of boundaries, asst. ch., office organization, ch., office of organization, dist. and asst. forester; ch. forester, comsn. servn. and bd. of rly. comsns., Canada, h. fire inspr., bd. of rly. comsns., 1921; loc. of American Foresters; successively, 1 sec., Can. Soc. of Forest Engnrs.

P. J., KNT. BACH. (1887), C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1832; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1858; graduate of the Faculty of Laws, Paris, 1858; ag. substitute procureur and advoc.-gen., Mauritius, in 1868, 1869, and 1870; mem. of the coun. of govt., 1869-1878; 1st puisne judge, 1879; ch. justice, 1883; ret., 1898.

LOGIE. HON. WILLIAM ALEXANDER, C.B. (1918).—B. 1866; ed. Queen's Univ., Kingston, Canada; B.A. (1st cl. hons. in classics), 1887; M.A., 1888; LL.B., 1892; barrister, Canada, 1890; bencher, Upper Canada, 1911; entered Vol. Militia, 1883, and is maj.-gen. comdg. 2nd Div., Toronto; puisne judge of high ct., div. of Ontario, and a judge of Appellate div., 1918.

LEESE, CHARLES WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1932).—Asst. dist. comsnr., S. Nigeria, Sept., 1906; transfd. to treasury dept., 1907; acted as supervisor of customs, 1908, and as junior asst. sec. in 1911; served as finan. offr., Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1916; dep. treas., 1921; ag. treas., May to Dec., 1921 and June to Dec., 1923; treas., G. Coast, 1924; ag. col. sec., Sept.-Nov., 1925 and from Oct., 1926 to Mar., 1927; gov.'s dep., on various occasions in 1926 and 1927; treas., Nigeria, Oct., 1928.

IMERE-GUPPY, P. (Junr.).—Super- ry clk., audit off., Trinidad, 1889; 4th b.-treasury., San Fernando, Jan., 1892; waiter, customs, May, 1894; clk.,assy., Mar., 1898; acted as warden, ma, in 1902, 1904 and 1905; sub-recvr., ter, collr. of cust. and harbr. mast., Oct., 1917; collected fresh-water fishes dad for British Museum. K, W. J., M.I.M.E—B. 1889; ed. St. bl., Cumberland; colliery man., Nigeria, PONTE, LOUIS.—B. 1881; mag., lesser encies, Mauritius, 1909; do., Mauritius brigues, Oct., 1913; ag. mast. and regir., 1913-14; ag. prot. of immigrts. and poor snr., 1914; war serv. in France and Mace- 916-19; food contr., May, 1920; puisne July, 1932; ag. ch. judge, sup. ct., Oct., EBORR, CLAUD MARIUS.—Served in S. Constab., 1901-7 (S.A. war med.); Bech. police, from 1907; sub-insp. Bech. Prot. 1st Apr., 1914; asst. res. mag.; passed v. serv. lower law exam. and 2nd grade na exam.; 2nd lieut., gen. list, 1919; ag. g., 1921; res. mag., 1921; passed Cape v. higher law exam., 1923; ag. asst. res. , 1930.
BATTLE, HAROLD RUNDLE FITZ.—B. 1875; d. offr., Basutoland, 1904.

LEECHMAN, ALLEYNE.—M.A. (Oxon.), F.L.S.; ed. at St. Paul's Schl.; natural science schl. of Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; ag. normal and agric. master, B. Guiana, 1907; ag. science lecturer, 1909; science lecturer, 1910; has acted as asst. dir. and as dir. of science and agric., B. Guiana; editor of "The Journal of the Board of Agriculture, B. Guiana" and of "The Handbook of B. Guiana, 1913"; deleg. to W. Indian agric. conf., Trinidad, 1911.
BATTLE, WILLIAM ROBERT.—B. 1871; med., Basutoland, 1896.

MINTER, K. S. — B. 1894; apptd. after npet. exam. asst. clk., and assigned to G.P.O., pt., 1912; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to M. stationery office, Aug., 1914; to C.O., st., 1914; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 22.

LEFEBVRE, PHILIP LLEWELLIN.—B. 1875; ed. at Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Brighton Coll., Sussex; clk., G.P.O., Cape Town, Feb., 1895; clk., atty.-gen.'s off., Feb., 1896; clk. to res. mag., Glen Grey, Feb., 1896; clk. to res. mag., East London, Sept., 1898; clk., sol.-gen.'s off., Grahamstown, Apr., 1899; clk., atty.-gen.'s off. (treason branch), Cape Town, Sept., 1900; B.A., Cape Univ., 1900; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., O.R.C., June, 1901; sec. to law dept., Oct., 1904; ag. clk. to legis. coun., May-July, 1906; relieving mag., 1912; mag., Thaba'Nchu, May, 1917; mag., Ladybrand, Feb., 1925; mag., Grahamstown, Feb., 1929; joint editor of law-book, O.R.C., 1900-1906.

LONDON. GEORGE ERNEST.—B. 1889; ed. at Warwick, and Downing Coll., Camb. (schol.); 1st in nat. sci. tripos, 1910; hist. tripos, 1911; B.A., 1911; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; offr., cls. V, 1914; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, 1916; on active serv., 1916-19; lieut., Glouc. Regt.; offr., cls. IV, 1919; sec. to res., Penang, 1920.

CECIL JOHN WOOD, M.A., D.D.—Late scholar and exhibitioner, St. Peter's Coll., Camb.; B.A. (2nd cl. class. tripos), 1896; M.A., 1901; D.D., Camb., 1912; deacon, 1897; priest, 1898; curate of Kent, 1897-99; St. Marylebone, 1899-1902; St. Andrew's, Bethnal Green, 1902-1906; consecrated, 14th July, 1912.

SIR THOMAS, G.C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1854; ent. N.Z. parl't., 1887; min. of industries and commerce and min. of agr., N.Z., 1910; prime min. and min. of lands, 1912; high comsnr. in London, 1912-20; mem. of comtee. for sttlmt. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; M.L.C., 1921.

MOOSONEE (BISHOP OF), RT. REV. JOHN HARDEN, D.D.—Schoolmaster and catechist under C.M.S., Moose factory, Hudson's Bay, 1851; ordained deacon and priest, 1852; Hud. Bay Co.'s chaplain, 1852; consecrated Bp. of Moosonee, 1872.

GALL.—B. 1870; apptd. asst. supt. of works, Straits Settlements, May, 1899; ag. supt. of works and surveys, Malacca, June, 1899, to Sept., 1901, and Oct., 1902, to Apr., 1903; supt. of works and surveys, Sing., July, 1903; ag. dep. col. engr. and survr.-gen., Penang, Nov., 1904, to Feb., 1905; ag. supt., wks. and survey, Malacca, May to Dec., 1906; ag. dep. col. engr. and survr.-gen., Penang, Jan., 1907.

EILL, J. H.—Med. offr., E. Africa Prot., , 1919; ditto, Uganda, July, 1920. EILL, JAMES SCOTT, B.A., Barrister-at- law.—B. 1889; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; clas. I., 1911; 1st sen. mod. hist. and pol. sci., ; Victoria prizeman, Kings Inns., Dublin; , Fiji, 1914; clk., leg. and exec. coun., , 1915; dist. comsnr., 1916; regr. sup. ct., off. recr., 1922; ag. atty. gen. and mem., and leg. coun., July, 1922 to July, 1923; prin. asst. col. sec. and agt. gen., immigr., , 1924; mem., bd. examrs., Fijian language ag. editor, Indian newspaper "Radjut"; ended sp. duty under Tongan govt. and apptd. n., privy coun. of Tonga, Aug.-Sept., 1924. ELSON, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. WILLIAM CHARLES SADLIER, M.A., D.D. of London; ; ed. Univ. of Melbourne; B.A. (Trin. ) 1894; M.A. 1896; Univ. of London, 1903; , London, 1909; deacon, 1891; priest, 1892, bourne; C.F., 1896; formerly curate of amid Hill, 1891-1892; St. Paul's, Bendigo, 1899; incumbent of Holy Trinity, East Mel- bourne, 1899-1904; lecturer, St. John's Coll., bourne, 1907-1910; canon of Melbourne Cathedral, 1901; examining chaplain to Bishops Bendigo and Gippsland, 1902; vicar of Christ- ch, St. Kilda, Melbourne, 1904; lecturer, ley College, Melbourne, 1910; consecrated bp. of Nelson, N.Z., 21st July, 1912.

AVARATNAM, SARAVANAMUTTU SAMUEL.— 1894; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; l., Matara Kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Ratnapura, May, 1922; extra office asst. to govt. agt., v. of Sabaragamuwa, Sept., 1923.
NEWTON, T. C.—Asst. res., N. Nigeria, 19th Dec., 1908. NIBLOCK-STUART, J.

McDONALD JOHN SCOTLAND.—Supt. of telephones, Antigua, May, 1895; also inspr. of weights and measures, May, 1906; ag. postmr., Dec., 1902 to Jan., 1903, and Mar. to May, 1903; trade and income tax assessor, 1907, 1909 and 1910; ag. chmn., bd. guardians, June, 1909, to Feb., 1910; city comsnr., 1910; J.P., Aug., 1910.

LLEWELLYN JONES, D.D. — B. 1840; ed. at Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1862; M.A., 1866; D.D. 1878; curate of Bromagrove, 1864-1874; rector of Little Hereford, with Ashford Carbonell, 1874-1878; consecr. bishop, 1878.
H.—Treasury asst., Kenya, Jan., 1917; senr. asst. treas., Nov., 1923.

REV. LLEWELLYN JONES, D.D. — B. 1840; ed. at Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1862; M.A., 1866; D.D. 1878; curate of Bromsgrove, 1864-1874; rector of Little Hereford, with Ashford Carbonell, 1874-1878; consec. bishop, 1878; died, 1918.

EALE, HAROLD BERNARD.—Second clk. to comsnr., Southern div., Bech. Prot., June, 1909; 2nd clk. to asst. comsnr., Northern Prot.; sed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1911; , 1912; ag. asst. res. mag., 1913; sub-inspr., h. Prot. pol., 1914; ag. res. mag., 1914; stated local rank of capt.; res. mag., 1921; sed 3rd grade Secwana exam., 1921.
NICHOLLS, HON. SIR HERBERT, Kt. Bt. (1916).—B. 1868; LL.B., Tasmania; called to the bar, 1892; atty.-gen. and min. of corr., Tasmania, 1903-4; puisne judge, Tasmania, 1909; chief justice, 1914.

MURRAY, SIR J. H. P., K.C.M.G. (1925), M.G. (1914)—M.A., Oxon.; ed. at Sydney Univ. schl. and won Knox prize, Fairfax prize, Univ. prize, and John West medal; went Brighton Coll., Germany, and Univ. Coll., London; entd. Oxford Univ. and gained foundation scholarship to Magdalen Coll., 1880; 1st cl. class. mods., 1882; 1st cl. Lit. Hum., 1884; B.A., 1885; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1886; practised as a barrister in Australia until aptd. crown prosecutor, N.S. Wales; acted as dist. ct. judge on several occasions; commanded [L.]S.W. Irish Rifles, 1898; served in S. African War, 1900-1901; recd. comn. of major in Impl. Army in recognition of his services, 1902; chief judicial offr. and mem. of exec. and legis. couns., Apus, Sept., 1904; ag. administr., 8th Apr., 1907; and lieut.-gov., in addition to duties of judge, 1908.

TAYLOR, HENRY WILLIAM LETTS, O.B.E. (D.).—B. 1873; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, R.N. Coll., Greenwich; Admiralty presenta- tion sword, 1893; R.M.L.I.; entd. crown agents' so., 1898; asst. head of gen. stores dept., 1903; head of shipping dept., 1912; head of insurance and checking dept., 1917; on loan to India Office and Miny. of Munitions, 1914-19; dir. of raw materials, 1918; asst. contr. of wage, 1918; head of shipping dept., 1st Jan., 1919; Chevalier de l'Ordre de la Couronne (Belgium).

NEWNHAM, HUBERT ERNEST.—B. 1886; ed. St. John's Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1909; office asst. to govt. agt., Uva, Nov., 1911; pol. mag., Matale, Mar., 1913; landing survr., Colombo customs, Oct., 1913; ag. comsnr. of requests, Colombo, Feb., 1920; asst. govt. agt., Colombo, May, 1920; chmn., munic. coun., Colombo, Aug., 1924.

NICHOLSON, WILLIAM EWART, M.D.—B. 1890; ed., Leeds Grammar Schl., and Keble Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, Sept., 1914; attached, Nigeria Regt., 1915; instr., arts and crafts, 1st Jan., 1920. NICHOLSON, LT.-GEN. SIR W. G. (1898); C.B. (1891).—B. 1845; entered Army, 1865; col., 1891; served in Afghan war (medal in desps.); Candahar field force, 1879 (medal in desps.); march to Candahar (desp. major, medal with 3 clasps, bronze medal); Egyptian war, 1882, including Tel-el-Kebir (clas. Osmanieh, Khedive's star); Burma campaign, 1886-7, as A.A.G. army headquarters (brevet lieut.-col., medal with clasp); Tibet expeditionary force, 1897-8, as chief of staff (medal with 2 clasps); served in S. Africa as mil. sec. to commdr.-in-chief and transport at headqrtrs., 1899-1900 (desp. promoted maj.-gen., medal with five clasps); dir.-gen. of mobilisation and mil. intelligence, 1901-4; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, Gibraltar, 1904-7; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, Malta, 1907-10; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, India, 1910-14; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, Egypt and the Sudan, 1914-17; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, Palestine and Transjordan, 1917-20; governor of the West Indies, 1920-23; atty.-gen. in Ferguson adminn., 1923; re-el. to Ont. legis. at by-el., 1922 and 1923; atty.-gen. in Ferguson adminn., 1923.
NICHOLS, JAMES EDWARD.—B. 1880; engnr., Lagos rly., Feb., 1907; asst. engnr. Coast rlys. (open lines), May, 1911; in charge, Togoland Mily. rlys., 1916-17; d. e. G. Coast rlys., Jan., 1920.

NICHOL, WALTER CAMERON.—B. 1866; journalist, "Spectator," Hamilton, 1881; Toronto "News" and Toronto "Saturday Night"; editor, Hamilton "Herald," 1888-96; "News," London, Ont., 1896-97; editor, Vancouver "Daily Province," 1898; proprietor and editor-in-ch., 1900; lieut.-gov. of British Columbia, 1921. NICHOLLS, HENRY ALFRED ALFORD, C.M.G. (1896), M.B. (Honours), C.M. (1873), M.D. Abdn. (1875); M.R.C.S. Eng., F.L.S. and C.M.Z.S.—B. 1851; corresp. mem. N. York Acad. of Sci., Jamaica Inst., centl. agric. bd. of Trinidad, and chamber of agric. of Guadeloupe; hon. mem. Roy. Agric. and Com. Soc. of Br. Guiana; Crown nominee in legis. assem. of Dominica, 1875-7; med. supt. of Dominica Yaws Hosp., 1877; surg., Roseau Infirm., 1879; med. offr., pub. institns., 1880; chmn., poor law bd., 1885-91; local comsnr., Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; ag. curator, Dominica Bot. Station, May to Oct., 1890; gained prize of 100L. for best text book of tropical agriculture offered by the govt. of Jamaica, 1890; special comsnr. to inquire into prevalence of Yaws in W. Indies, 1891, for report on which (pub. as Blue Book) recd. thanks of S. of S., 1894; J.P., 1896; chmn. of the town bd. of Roseau, 1896-98; hlth. offr. of Dominica, 1897; offl. mem. of legis. coun., 1898; represented Leeward Is. at W. Indian agric. confce. in Barbados, 1899, 1901 and 1902; sen. med. offr., Dominica, 1904; rep. Leeward Is. at W. Indian quarantine confce. in Barbados, 1904, and at W. Indian agric. confce. in Trinidad, 1905; chmn., permanent exhibn. comtee., 1906; trustee, free library, 1910; chmn., bd. of dirs. of Victoria museum, 1910; pres. of legis. coun., Dominica, 23rd Feb., 1911; mem. exec. coun., 1914; ch. of coronation celebrations comtee., 1911; com. for Dominica rubber and tropical products exh. London, 1914; represented Dominica at internat. congress on trop. agric., London; represented Leeward Is. at Imperial health congress, London, 1914; mem. food prices com., 1914; chrmn., roads enquiry comsn., 1918; ch. quarantine bd., 1919; admstd. govt. of Dominica, Feb.-June, 1919, Oct., 1919 to May, 1921, Feb., 1921, Mar.-Apr., 1922 and Mar., 1923; offl. mem. exec. coun., Leeward Is.; prin. med. offr., Dominica, 1922.
REGAN, JOHN WILLIAM HAMILTON, B.A. on)—B. 1913; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., i.

NICHOLLS, LUCIUS.—B. 1884; L.S.A., M.D., B.C., B.A. Cantab.; pathologist to some hosp. socy., and bacteriologist to post-grad. schl. of clinical med., 1907-8; stationed in Indies, 1909-12, as bacteriologist to gov. St. Lucia and surg. to Victoria hosp.; S. Africa, 1913-15; dir., bact. institute, 1915; ag. med. supt. lun. asyl. in addition to his own duties, May, 1916 to Feb., 1917.

ETELTON, GERALD ENBRIGHT.—Clk., res. snr.'s office, 1914; sub-inspr., Bechuanaland pol., 1917; sp. J.P., 1917; ag. asst. res. , 1920; passed 1st grade Secwana exam., 1920; passed civ. serv. lower law exam., 1922; ag. res. mag., 1923; res. mag. and hon. inspr. Bech. Prot. pol., Apr., 1924.

SIR FREDERICK ROBERT, P.C., K.C.M.G. (1911), LL.D., D.C.L. — Mem. of Natal parlmt. 1886-1910; min. for native affairs, 1893-97; ditto, 1899-1903; Natal deleg. to cust. union conf. at Cape Town, 1898; Natal deleg. at inauguration of Australian Commonwealth, 1901; ag. prime min. during Sir Albert Hime's absence in England for the Coronation, 1902; prime min., Nov., 1906; represented the colony at Impl. confce., 1907; deleg. to England in connection with passing of S. African Act through Impl. parlmt., 1909; min. of comm. and industry, 1910; nominated mem. of senate, Union of S. Africa; ceased to be mem., senate, 1924.

MATHER NORMAN FREDERICK HUGH.—B. 1890; ed. Merchant Taylor's Schl., and Hertford Coll., Oxford (schol.); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; served in Rifle Brig., Dec., 1915–Sept., 1919; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Nov., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Klang, Mar., 1921; asst. dist. offr., Larut, Jan., 1922; registr., war serv. land grant scheme, S. Sttlmts., Feb., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Larut, Aug., 1922; asst. dist. offr., Larut, Sept., 1922; sec. to res., Pahang, Dec., 1923; mag., Seremban, July, 1925; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; ag. state treas., Negri Sembilan, in addn., Jan., 1926; chmn., sany. bd., Seremban, Aug., 1926; ag. asst. adviser, Kota Tinggi, Apr., 1928; ag. asst. advr., Batu Pahat, Apr., 1928; ditto, Segamat, May, 1928.
O'MALLEY. A. H.—b. 1906; ed. Cork Gram. Sch. and Trinity Coll., Dublin mil.; serv., 1931–36, surg. lt.; I.M.S., 1937, lt.-col.; ophthalmic specialist, Tang., 1950.
REILLY, HARRY ALLAN OSWALD.—B. 1893; ed to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1924 (cert. of hon.); . registr. gen., Trinidad, 1934; atty.-gen. and sr., sup. ct., St. Vincent, 1936.

HENRY, C.M.G. (1906).—and class clk., impl. customs, Belfast, Feb., 1875; served at Cork and Plymouth; selected for special service with survrs.-gen., and in connection with reorganisation of customs statistical dept., 1893; 2nd offr., Folkestone, 1894; collr. of customs, Trinidad, Dec., 1895; mem. legis. coun., 1898; one of the representatives of Trinidad at commercial conferences at Washington and Ottawa, 1900; apptd. to exec. coun. 1903; del. for Trinidad at W. Indian quarantine confce., 1904; mem. West Indian central quarantine authority, 1907; ret. on pension, 1908; visited Malta to inquire into working of customs dept., 1908, and re-organized dept. in course of subsequent visits, 1909-10; apptd. govt. dir. of Trinidad Dock and Engineering Co., 1908; technical adviser to roy. comsn. on trade relations between Canada and W. Indies, 1909-1910.

NICHOLSON, BERTRAM, C.B.E. (1923).—B. 1875; Anglo-Bar., 1899-1901; served six years in Natal civ. serv.; sub-native comsnr., Hlatikulu, Swaziland, 1902 to Mar., 1907; asst. res. mag., Hlatikulu, 1907; asst. comsnr., Hlatikulu, 22nd Mar., 1907; served S.W. African campaign, 1914-15; res. comsnr. and govt. sec., Swaziland, 1st Jan., 1920.
OSBORNE, RICHARD BOURKE.—Cadet, S. tlmts., Nov., 1909; ag. dep. regisr. of sup. ct., nang, May, 1910.

OTTERSON, HENRY.—B. 1846; apptd. to the staff of H. of Rep., N. Zealand, 1872; clk. of Rep. since 1898. OTTLEY, REAR-ADMIRAL SIR CHARLES LANGLEY, K.C.M.G. (1907); M.V.O. (1903).—B. 1858; eru. 1877; Egypt, 1882; has served as naval attaché to maritime cts. at Washington, Tokio, Rome, St. Petersburg, and Paris; dir. of naval intell., 1905-1907; sec. to comtee. of Imp. defence, 1907; is also naval A.D.C. to H.M. the King.

NICOLLS, EDWARD HUGH DYNELT.—B. 1871; A.M.I.C.E., 2nd cl. certif. and bracketed first in All England in geom. and perspective drawing, Camb. loc. exams., 1886; upt. of pub. wks., Cyprus, May, 1898; 1st div. engnr., Dec., 1898; ag. dir. of pub. wks., Mar., 1903; dir. of pub. wks., Jan., 1904; O/c., A.S.C., Cyprus, in addition to own duties, June, 1915, to Nov., 1916; O/c., R.E., from commencement of he war to date of leaving Cyprus on apptmt. as ir. of pub. wks., Gold Coast, 22nd Nov., 1918; ranted local and tempy. rank of capt. from 1st July, 1916, to date of leaving Cyprus; ment. in esps.

OSWELL, WILLIAM ST. JOHN, F.R.G.S.—Ed. at Oswestry gram. schl. and by private tutor; out. 2nd batt. sharpshooters, Boer war, 1901-2 (medal and 5 clasps); asst. comsnr., S. Leone, 1903; comsnr., Karene dist., S. Leone Prot., 1906. O'TOOLE, W. F.—Entd. Bahamas civ. serv., 1893; 2nd clk., post office, 1904; Out Island comsnr. (2nd div.), 1909.
O'SULLIVAN, HON. THOS.—B. 1856; solr., Queensland, 1878; called to the bar; sec. for b. wks., Queensland, 1907; min. of agric., with seat in legis. coun., Feb., 1908.

OVERY, CHAS. RAND.—Educ. at Yalding ram. schl.; ch. architectural draughtsman, admiralty wks. dept., Keyham naval barracks, 1903-4; ch. draughtsman, survey dept., E.A.P., 1904-6; govt. architect, P.W.D., E.A.P., 15th July, 1908. OWEN, SIR WM., Kt.
BACH. (1906).—B. 1834; D.C.L., 1882; chief judge in equity, N.S. Wales, 1887-1896; senior puisne judge until his retirement from the bench in 1908.
PADDLE, JAMES ISAAC.—F.R.C.S. (Eng.); M.D. (Lond.); asst. govt. med. offr. and comsnr. in lunacy, Mauritius, 8th Sept., 1890; med. supt., lunatic asylum, 15th June, 1898.

OUIMET, THE HON. JOSEPH ALDRIC, K.C.—Speaker of the House of Commons of Canada, 1890-91; min. of pub. wks., 1892-6; judge, ct. of appeal, Montreal, May, 1896; ret., 1906; mem. of privy coun. for Canada.
PEREZ, JOSEPH ALDRIC, M.D. Edin.—B. 1860; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, 6th Apl. 1887; dist. med. offr., 1st Mar., 1915.

PACE BARDON, C.—Entd. Malta civ. ser., 1884; clk., 1886; dep. supt. of pub. wks., 1899; dep. aud.-gen., 1905; rec.-gen. and dir. of contracts, with seat in exec. coun. and seat in coun. of govt., 1910.

OSBORNE, MAJOR D. R.—B. 1879; ed. ince Alfred Coll., Adelaide, S. Australia, delaide Univ. (mental and moral phil.) and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., law; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1909; practiced at bar until 1914; on mily. serv., 1914-19; ct. martial offr., Nov., 1918 to May, 1920; in Ireland as A.L.O. until Apr., 1922; stip. and circuit mag., Bahamas, June, 1923; ag. ch. just., Aug. to Sept., 1923 and from July to Nov., 1924; ag. reg.-gen. in addn. June to July, 1924.

NEWTON, SIR FRANCIS JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1892), C.V.O. (1911).—B. 1857; ed. at Rugby and Univ. Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1880; M.A., 1890; barrister, Inner Temple; lieut., 3rd batt. Hampshire regt., and extra A.D.C. to Sir H. Robinson, gov. Cape, 1881; priv. sec. to Sir Thomas Scanlen, prime minister of Cape, 1883; and to Sir H. Robinson, 1884-86; sec. to Mauritius roy. comsn., 1886; ag. admstr. and ch. mag., Br. Bechuanaland, 1888; col. sec. and rec.-gen., 1889; ag. admstr. and ch. mag., 1892; res. comsnr., Bechuanaland Protectorate, Dec., 1895-7; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 1898; ditto, Barbados, 1901; treas., S. Rhodesia, 1903; ret., 1919; col. sec., 1st S. Rhodesian ministry, 1st Oct., 1923; high comsnr. for S. Rhodesia in London. NEWTON, HENRY HIBBERT.—B. 1861; entd. law dept., Victoria, 1880; joined staff of legis. assem., 1884; clk. asst., 1902; clk. of legis. assem., 1910; and clk. of parlmts.
PERERA, THEODORE DUNCAN.—B. 1895; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., local divn., Aug., 1919; attchd. to Kurunegala Kach., Aug., 1919; Jaffna Kach., Dec., 1919.

PERKINS, ARTHUR JAMES.—B. 1871; Diplômé de l'école Nationale d'Agriculture de Montpellier, 1890; management of farm estates, Tunia, 1890-2; prof. of viticulture and oenology, S. Aust., 1892; sec. for agriculture, 1902-1904; prin. of agric. coll. and prof. of viticulture and oenology, 1904; dir. of agric., 1914.
PERTH, ARCHBISHOP OF, MOST REV. CHAS. OWEN LEAVER RILEY, D.D., Cantab.—B. 1854; vicar of St. Paul's, Preston, Lanca., 1885-1894; bishop of Perth, W. Aust., 1894; created archbishop, 1914.

RMSBY-GORE, RT. HON. WILLIAM GEORGE rhur, P.C. (1927).—B. 1885; ed. Eton t New Coll., Oxford (B.A.); late capt., opshire Yeomanry; intell. officer Arab eau, 1916-17; asst. sec., War Cabinet, 1917- asst. polit. offr., Palestine, 1918; British m., Permanent Mandates Comsn., L. of N., 1-22; M.P., Stafford since Jan., 1910; ompanied C.O. mission to British West lies, 1921-22; parly. under sec. of state Colonies, 1st Nov., 1922 to 23rd Jan., 4 and since Nov., 1924; chmn., parly. comsn. E. Africa, 1924; apptd. by S. of S. to visit and t. on W. African dependencies, Jan., 1926; of S. for the Cols., 1936.

PERRY, AYLESWORTH BOWEN, C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1860; barrister; ed., Royal Mily. Coll., Kingston; lieut., R.E., 20th July, 1880; resigned 31st Aug., 1881; joined R.N.W.M. Pol., 24th Jan., 1882; comsnr., 1st Aug., 1900; served, N.W. Rebellion. PERRY, B. C. G.—B. 1886; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to C.O., 9th Apr., 1906. PERRY, GEORGE McREDDIE, M.B., C.M., Glasgow, and St. Mary's hosp., London, F.R.G.S.—Asst. col. surg., Lagos, Oct., 1893; ag. col. surg., Feb.-Mar., 1894; ag. asst. col. sec., May, 1894; med. offr. to expedn. to Idanan mountains: sen. asst. col. surg., Aug., 1895; ag. col. surg., 1895.

PAGE, GEORGE WM.—B. 1861; copyist, science and art dept., S. Kensington museum, Apr., 1881, to Dec., 1884; 2nd cl. clk. prsn. dept., Jan., 1885, to Dec., 1896; keeper of the gaol Freetown, S. Leone, Jan., 1897, to Apr., 1901; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Leone Prot., May, 1901; dist. comsnr., Oct., 1903; dist. comsnr. of Sherbro dist., Aug., 1905, and in addition dist. comsnr. of N. Sherbro dist., Mar., 1906; ag. curator of intestate estates, Nov., 1900, to Apr., 1901; is J.P. for the colony.

ORR, LIONEL ALEXANDER WILLIAM.—B. 1905; . St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin and Trinity Coll., blin, B.A., LL.B.; solr., sup. ct., I.F.S.; st. comsnr., lands, Gold Coast, Aug., 1929 Aug., 1932; asst. admstr.-gen., N. Rhodesia, ig., 1932; ag. admstr.-gen., ag. offl. recr., ag. gisr., high ct., ag. registr. of companies, ag. gisr. of patents, trade marks and designs, ag. gisr. of deeds, Mar.-June, 1934; dep. registr., gh ct., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1934; registr., ., 1936.

REILLY, JAMES GALLUS.—B. 1883; ed. ghes' Acad., Belfast; R.I.C., May, 1902; r., pol., F.M.S., Dec., 1910; inspr., wghts, mars., Aug., 1911-Dec., 1912; supvr., cust.. I.S., Sept., 1921; supt., cust. prev. br., I.S., Oct., 1923; supvr., expt. duties, F.M.S., i and S'ore, Nov., 1925 and Jan., 1927; ch. t., prev. br., cust. dept., F.M.S., Sept., 1927. . supt., govt. monops., S'pore, June, 1930; comsnr., trade and cust., N. Sembilan, Feb., 2; ditto, Perak, Nov., 1932; do., Selangor . Pahang, Oct., 1933; asst. supt., govt. nops., S.S., Penang, Nov., 1934.

C.E., A.R.I.B.A.—Divsnl. survr. and engnr. for county of Middlesex: dist. engnr. for City dist. of Bristol; ditto for Clifton dist., Bristol; exec. engnr. P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1904; 1st asst., D.P.W., 1918; ag. D.P.W., Sept. to Nov., 1918. PERLEY, HON.
PETCH, TOM, B.A., B.Sc., (Lond.).—B. 1870; botanist and mycologist, Ceylon, 1905; ag. dir. of agric., 1914, 1915 and 1916.

PAGDEN, A. S.—Ed. at Wellington Coll. and King's Coll., Camb., scholar of King's Coll., 1st class, classical tripos, 1881; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1881; pol. mag., Panadure, June, 1887; office asst. to the govt. agt., Colombo, Dec., 1890; dist. judge, Badulla, 1896; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, Mar., 1896; ag. comsnr. of requests, Colombo, June, 1896; pol. mag., Colombo, June, 1900; chmn., municipal coun., and mayor of Colombo, June, 1901; prin. asst. col. sec., Feb., 1905; postmr.-gen., Nov., 1906.

PERIES, FRANCIS DANIEL.—B. 1864; ed. Royal coll., Colombo; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., 1909; pol. mag., S. Prov., Jan., 1904, Apr., 1905; Mar., 1907; ag. comsnr. of requeste and pol. mag., Galle, Mar., 1905; ag. pol. mag., Balapitiya, Oct., 1906; pol. mag., Chilaw, July, 1910; pol. mag., Panadura, Feb., 1912; ag. dist. jud., Tangalla, Oct., 1913; dist. jud., Ratnapura, July, 1917.

PERKINS, LEICESTER GRANT.—B. 1890; ed. privately and Harrison Coll., Barbados; clk., post office, Barbados, Jan., 1909; 5th clk., audit office, Mar., 1911; 2nd clk., registrn. office, Nov., 1913; clk. to master in chancery, May, 1914; 4th clk., savings bank, Jan., 1915; 2nd-lieut., Imperial Army, 30th Mar., 1917, served during the war in Egypt and Palestine with 2nd B.W.I. Regt.; 1st-lieut., 1st Oct., 1918; 1st clk., Barbados savings bank, Aug., 1919; 2nd grade supvr. of customs, Nigeria, Feb., 1920. PERKINS, THOS. LUFF, A.M. Inst.

SIR GEORGE HALSEY, K.C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1857; ed. Ottawa gram. schl., St. Paul's schl., Concord, N.H., and Harvard Univ.; B.A., 1878; elec. to H. of C., Canada (Argenteuil) 1904; re-elec., 1908 and 1911; chief conservative whip, 1911; mem. of privy coun. for Canada and min. without portfolio in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; ag. high comsnr. in London, 1914; min. of overseas mil. forces, 1916; high comsnr., 1917; joint comsnr. and plenipotentiary of Canada to sign Peace Treaties, 1919. PERRY, SIR ALLAN, Kt. BACH. (1904); M.D. (Durham); D.P.H.(Lon.); M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.S.A. Lond.—B. 1860; prin. civ. med. offr. and inspr.-gen. of hosps., Ceylon; prin. of Ceylon med. coll., 10th Dec., 1897; major R.A.M.C. (retired); mem. municipal coun., Colombo; late pres. Ceylon branch Br. med. assoc.; del. for Ceylon to Internat. Leprosy Conf., Bergen, 1909; ret., 1915.

PETRIDES, PHILIP BERTIE.—B. 1881; ed. Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1906; joined Midland Circ. and Birmingham and cent. crim. court sess., 1906; legal adviser and crown prosecutor, Seychelles, 22nd Jan., 1916; ex-officio mem. exec. and legis. couns.; ag. chief just., 23rd July, 1916, to 20th Mar., 1917, 13th May, 1918 to 8th Oct., 1918, and from 23rd Dec., 1918 to 11th Nov., 1919; temp. empld., C.O., Feb. to Apr., 1920; ch. just., Seychelles, 10th June, 1920.
PRITCHARD, WILLIAM OWEN.—Med. offr., Africa Prot., 15th Feb., 1904.

RR, SIR CHARLES WILLIAM JAMES, K.C.M.G. 28), C.M.G. (1921).—B. 1870; ed. Bath Coll. i Woolwich Acad.; 1st comsn., Royal Artillery, Feb., 1889; capt., Sept., 1899; maj., 4; ret., Sept., 1908, served with Chitral ief force, 1895 (medal with clasp); Indian ntier, 1897 (clasp); Tirah expedit. force, 1897- (clasp); China war, 1900-01 (medal); S. rican war, 1900-02 (medal, 2 clasps); 3rd cls. ., N Nigeria, Feb., 1903; 2nd cls. res., 1904; . cls. res., 1908; ag. sec. to admnstr., N. Nigeria, y to Sept., 1907; ch. sec., Cyprus, Feb., 1911; mnstd. govt., July to Oct., 1911, and Apr. to ag., 1913; ret. on pension on account of ill th, Feb., 1917; attd. W.O., Int. branch, 1918- ; col. sec., Gibraltar, June, 1919; ag. gov., on veral occasions: gov. and commdr.-in-ch. of the hamas, 1926-1931; ret.

PERRY-LEWIS, CAPT. GEORGE.—F.S.S.; B. 1880; entd. Imp. postal serv., 1st July, 1896; transfd. to post and telegraph dept., E. Africa and Uganda Prot., 3rd Nov., 1905; asst. P.M.G., N. Nigeria, 27th Apr., 1910; ag. dep. P.M.G., Jan., 1913; 1st cls. postmr., E.A.P., Nov., 1913; ag. asst. P.M.G., Nov., 1916; asst. P.M.G., Mar., 1918; passed govt. test in Swahili language; lieut., E. Africa (field) telegraph sect., Nov., 1916; ag. capt. and offr. commdng., July, 1918.

PETRIE, WILLIAM VERNON.—B. 1884; asst. to res. engnr., South extn., Lagos rly., Sept., 1905; asst. engnr., Lagos rly. (open lines), May, 1907; asst. engnr., Gold Coast rlys., Jan., 1911; dist. engnr., Jan., 1917; ch. engnr., July, 1919; dep. gen. man., Jan., 1920. PETTIT, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.I. Mech.

PHILBRICK, ARTHUR JAMES.—Ed. Rugby, 1879-1884, Trin. Coll., Camb., 1884-1887; B.A.; LL.B., Camb.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1889; asst. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 16th Nov., 1901; prov. comsnr., 14th Apr., 1902; prov. comsnr., Ashanti, 1900; ag. chief comsnr., Ashanti, 1911, 1912 and 1914.
PRITCHARD, MAJOR C. G.—1st comsn. R.A., d Nov., 1891; promoted capt., 4th Dec., 1899; ., Hong Kong volunteers, Jan. to July, 1901; ndt. and adj. (local major), 2nd Apr., 1902.

PHELIPS, HUGH RICHARD.—Ed. Weymouth Coll. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; local auditor, Niger Coast Prot., 27th Oct., 1894; asst. auditor, E. Africa Prot., 9th Dec., 1896; local auditor, Uganda, 29th Apr., 1897; served in Uganda mutiny (medal and clasp); local auditor, E. Africa and Uganda rly., 26th Dec., 1901; auditor, Hong Kong, 1st Nov., 1904; J.P., 1905; hon. auditor, Hong Kong Univ., 2nd May, 1911.

PRINSEP, HENRY CHARLES.—Ed. Cheltenham ll; went to W. Australia, 1866; apptd. J.P. 1867; ag. clk. and draftsman in lands dept., '4; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to admstr., Nov., 1884, June, 1885; ch. draftsman, lands dept., 1890; clk, 1892; under-sec. for mines, 1894; ch. prot. aborigines, May, 1898.

PUGHE, S. R.—B. 1877; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser., and assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1897; promoted, with gratuity, Aug., 1904; confl. clk., Dec., 1907; minor staff clerk, 1911.

PHILBRICK, ARTHUR JAMES.—Ed. Rugby, 1879-1884, Trin. Coll., Camb., 1884-1887; B.A.; LL.B., Cambs.; called to the bar, Middle Temp., 1889; asst. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 16th Nov., 1901; prov. comsnr., 14th Apr., 1902; prov. comsnr., Ashanti, 1900; ag. chief comsnr., Ashanti, 1911, 1912 and 1914; attached to War Trade dept., London, Feb.-Sept., 1915, and from May to Sept., 1917. PHILIPPS, JAMES ERASMUS TRACY, Capt. R. of O., M.C. (1916), B.A., B.

PRITCHETT, CECIL JOHN SUCKLING, B.A. (con.).—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civil serv., Dec., 2; attached to Jaffna Kachcheri, Jan., 1913; pol. mag., Trincomalee, July, 1913; ag. asst. ltmt. offr., Nov., 1913; attached to Jaffna chcheri, Aug., 1914. PROBYN, SIR LESLIE, K.C.M.G. (1909); M.G. (1903).—Ed. at Charterhouse, and in ance and Germany, called to the bar, d. Tem., 1884; joint author of "The risdiction and Practice of the Mayor's Court," i other law books; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, B; atty.-gen. of Grenada, May, 1896; also ag. .sec. in 1897-8; ag. admstr. on several occasions; . secretary, S. Nigeria, 12th Apr., 1901; gov., era Leone, 14th July, 1904; gov., Barbados, i Nov., 1910; assumed govt., 13th Feb., 1911.

PRIOR, WM. BURGESS.—Mem. provisional N. neo Assoc.; res. E. Coast dist. Br. N. Borneo, g., 1881; prot. of coolies, E Coast, 1884; m. E. Coast coun., 1883; Br. consular agt., 1880; ..and mag., Gold Field dist., Feb., 1887.

PRENTIS, JOHN BERNARD.—B. 1904; ed. som Coll. and Guy's Hosp.; admitted solr., sup. of judicature, Eng., 1932; priv. prac. Hong ng, 1932-35; junr. legal offr., Hong Kong, ly, 1935; J.P., 1935; ag. asst. land offr. and p. regr., marriages, July, 1935; asst. cr. solr., ec. 1935; ag. asst. land offr. and dep. regr., arriages in addn., Jan., 1936; ag. offl. recr. d regr.. trade marks and letters pat., Mar., 36.

PRYCE, HOWARD LLOYD, C.M.G. (1911).— Sv. comsnr., Gambia Prot., Oct., 1896; attached Gambia field force, 1901 (medal and clasp); . ch. mag., Gambia, July to Oct., 1903; 1st cls. sv. comsnr., Jan., 1904.

PRESCOTT, ARTHUR STANLEY.—Apptd. after mpet. exam., supplementary clk., supreme ct., ibraltar, 1895; 3rd cls. clk., 1899; asst. registr. d deputy clk. of arraigns, 1911. PRETHEROE, EDWARD OWEN, M.C., B.A.; L.B.—B. 1896; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1921; lmstve. offr., 1922; asst. commr., lands, 1924, g. commr., lands, 1924; asst. sec., S. Provs.. 924; pol. mag., 1928; ag. ch. registr., sup. ct. on various occasions; crown counl., 1930; ag. solr.-gen. on various occasions.

E.—B. 1866; ed. Battersea Gram. Schl.; served pupilage with Messrs. John Aird and Sons, 1881-1886; engnr. and man. in charge of construction of various pub. wks., in U.K., 1886 to 1910; chief asst. engnr., govt. water works, G. Coast, 1910; ag. res. engnr. on ditto, 1910 to 1916; dep. D.P.W., G. Coast, Feb., 1916; ag. D.P.W., Mar. to Sept., 1916, and from 4th Oct. to 13th Dec., 1916; temporarily attached to Ministry of Munitions in England from 22nd Feb., 1917 to 31st Jan., 1918; ag. dir. of pub. wks., and M.L.C., 28th Mar., 1918.

PURDON, ANDREW.—B. 1875; ed. at Ebury and Conway colls., N. Wales; cadet, H.M.S. "Conway"; sub.-lieut., R.N.R., Apl., 1905; lieut., 1908; qualified at Whale Island and H.M.S. "Vernon"; asst. marine supt., N. Nigeria Marine, 11th Sept., 1909; whilst on leave took special course of instruction in hydrographical survey at R.N. coll., Greenwich, 1911.

PTOLEMY, WM. JOHN, I.S.O., J.P.—B. 1850; .. at Smithville gram. schl., Ontario; served ith G.W. Rly. Co. of Canada, 1873-1875; P.R. Dominion govt. telegraph construction, 75; mercantile life, 1876-1882; entd. govt. rv. as acctnt., treasury dept., Manitoba, 1883 to 385; municipal comsnr., 1886; dep. prov. ear., 1886 to date.
UTLEDGE, GEORGE ANTHONY LEY.—B. 3; ed. Sherborne schl. and Worcester Coll., Ord, B.A. 1935; Territorial Army (R. A.) 3-35; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1935.

READ. THE VEN. ARCH. J. H., D.D. — 1st ARCHDEACON OF PRINCE EDWARD ISLAND, 1860. — Ecclesiastical commissary, 1858; formerly of St. John's College, Cambridge, and B.D., 1852; D.D., 1857, of King's College, Frederickton, New Brunswick; rector of Milton, 1865.
ABINE, NEVILLE WARDE, B.A.—B. 1910; Chorlton-cum-Hardy Manchester Gram- r Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; col. dist dept., Feb., 1934; asst. audr., Gold Coast, r., 1934.

PURCELL, GILBERT KENHELM TREFFRY.— Ed. Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1888; M.A. 1893; called to bar, Linc.'s Inn, Nov., 1890; mem. of western circ., also practised at cent. crim. ct.; apptd. asst. to atty.-gen. Leeward Islds. (St. Kitts), Mar., 1898; official mem. of exec. and legis. couns. of the presidency of St. Christopher and Nevis; ag. att.-gen. Leewards, Aug.-Oct., 1900; apptd. ag. 1st puisne judge, June, 1901; puisne judge, G. Coast, 11th June, 1901; chief justice, Sierra Leone, 1911.

(1910), K.B.E. (1918).—B. 1853; formerly major in command of 1st Queenslanders, and senior offr. in Cent. Queensland; man. and dir. of various public companies; P., Queensland, 1887; pres. of Rockhampton Chamber of commerce; Queensland comsnr. for Franco-British exhibn.; agt.-gen. for Queensland, 1910; mem. of comtee. for settmt. of ex-serv. men within the Empire, 1917.

PROUT, W. T., C.M.G. (1905), M.B., C.M. (din.).—Asst. poor law med. offr., Mauritius, 35; surg. supt., immigrant ship "Dundee," 37; asst. col. surg., Gold Coast, 1888; dist. nsnr., Gold Coast, 1890; col. surg., Gambia, 3; ag. chief mag., Gambia, 1893; med. r. in charge of base hosp. during campaign against Foday Sila; col. surg., S. Leone, 1895, title vered to prin. med. offr., 1901; retired, 1906; low of roy. med. soc., Edin., and corrpdg. sm. of the epidemiological soc. of Lond.; lect., il. of trop. med., Univ. of Liverpool; med. visor to C.O.; mem. of advisory, med. and litary comtee. for Tropical Africa, C.O.; thor of article "Yaws" in "Diseases of Warm mates" (Davidson); Fil. Voloulous (Archives Parasitologie, May, 1901); "Filariasis in S. one" (Brit. Med. Journal, 1902); and other pers.

RAJA MUSA BIN RAJA BOT.—Malay probr., F.M.S., Oct., 1910; Malay offr., grade III, July, 1914; Malay asst., ditto, grade II, July, 1917; ditto, grade I, July, 1920; offr., cls. V, Malayan civ. serv., Jan., 1924; ag. asst. collr. land rev., Seremban, Jan., 1927; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1928.

UTHERFOORD, JOHN WYNNE CORRIE (Oxon).—B. 1888; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Ton Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Nigeria, 1911; seconded as asst. recr., 1914; asst. Nigerian secretariat, Lagos, 1926; clk., exec. leg. couns., 1926; cls. I, grade I, admstve.., 1928. UTHERFORD, CAPT. PETER WILLIAM, M.O.
6), F.R.G.S.—B. 1894; ed. Brighton Coll.; lieut., W. Yorks Regt., 1914; lieut., 1916; served, Flying Corps, 1916; capt., 1917; retd. from army, 1919; asst. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, t., 1921.

PUGSLEY, HON. WILLIAM, K.C., D.C.L.— B. 1850; educ. schls., Sussex, N.B., Univ. of New Brunswick, B.A. (1868), and Univ. of Lond., B.C.L., D.C.L.; called to the bar, N.B., 1872; reporter of the supreme ct., 1873-83; Q.C. by Dom. govt., 1891; elec. to legis., N.B., 1885; speaker, 1887; solr.-gen., 1890; resigned, 1892; re-entered legis., 1899; attorney-gen., 1900; premier, 1907; sworn of the priv. council for Canada and min. of pub. wks., 1907; re-elect. to H. of C., Sept., 1911; resig. portfolio on defeat of Laurier govt., Oct., 1911.

SAINT, SYDNEY JOHN, B.Sc., A.I.C..—B. 1897; Beauminster Grammar Schl., Reading Univ.; Sc., 1920; served with R.A.F., 1916-19; Salters research fellow, 1920-22; lect. in agrl. chemistry, eds Univ., 1922-26; agrl. chemist, Barbados, 1927; dist. dir. and chem., dept., sci. and agr., 1936.

RAJA UDA BIN RAJA MUHAMMAD.—B. 1894; Malay probr., F.M.S., Oct., 1910; Malay asst., grade III, Sept., 1913; ditto, grade II, July, 1917; ditto, grade I, July, 1920; offr., cls. V, Malayan civ. serv., Jan., 1924; ag. 2nd mag., Kuala Lumpur, Mar., 1924; ag. 1st mag., Kuala Lumpur, in addn., Apr., 1926; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kroh, Nov., 1927; offr. cls. IV., Jan., 1928; 2nd mag., Kuala Lumpur, Jan., 1928; ag. dist. offr. Raub, April, 1928; 2nd mag., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1928.

ST. JULIAN, HERBERT PAUL.—B. 1866; clk., postal dept., Fiji, June, 1883; clk., G.P.O., Aug., 1886; clk. and acctnt., Aug., 1888; ag. col. postmr., Jan., 1905; col. postmr., Sept., 1905; M.L.C., Oct., 1913; J.P., Feb., 1917.

Litt., F.R.G.S., F.R.A.I.—B. 1888; ed. Marlborough Coll., Oxford and Durham Univs., of which was undergraduate representative at the Congress of Univs. of the Empire, London, 1912; editor of Durham Univ. Journal; hon. sec., D.U.B.C.; pres. of the Union; lieut. (res. of offrs.), 1913; asst. dist. comsnr. and addnl. dist. magis., Uganda, 27th Mar., 1913; asst. intelligence offr., E. Africa Expedy. Force, from 8th Aug., 1914, attached K.A.R.; commanding, 2000 armed levies and scouts, from 9th Sept., 1914 (wounded and ment. in desps.); admnstd. Kifumbiro (Bukoba) area of occupied territory from 5th Sept., 1914, under martial law; polit. offr. on staff of Major-Gen. J. M. Stewart, C.B., Bukoba Expedy. Forces, from 15th June, 1915 (ment. in desps.); captnd., Kagera Command Staff, Jan., 1916; chief polit. offr., Uganda area, including Kagera mil. dist., from 17th Jan., 1916; chief intelligence offr. (C.S.O.3), on staff of the Lake Command, E.A.E.F., to Tabora, from 4th Aug., 1916; Mily. Cross, 1916; in charge of polit. mission in British and Belgian areas of conquered territory, Nov., 1916, to Mar., 1917, when invalided; empl. intell. duties, W.O., June to Aug., 1917; attached Admiralty War Staff, intell. div., Aug. to Oct., 1917; employed by F.O. on mission to Abyssinia (attached Rifle Brigade), Jan., 1918; on special duty in Sudan, Jan., 1918; raised two companies Somalis and Abyssinians for K.A.R. in French and Abyssinian territory, Apr., 1918; ag. dist. cmsnr., Kigezi, British Ruanda, and dist. mag. Kabale, Feb., 1919; publications, "Pan-Islam in Africa," "Cape to Cairo developments"; "Ruanda" (1919); "The Nabingi" (1919); "A Kinyar Ruanda Vocabulary" (1920); "Ethiopianism" (1920).

ST. LAURENT, ARTHUR, C.E.—Grad. of Polytechnic schl., Montreal, 1885; b. 1859; apptd. draftsman, Dominion pub. wks., Canada, 1888; asst. engnr., Winnipeg residency, for Manitoba and N.W.T., 1890; prin. asst. engnr. for Dominion pub. wks., 1897; engnr. in charge of Georgian Bay ship canal surveys, 1904; asst. ch. engnr., 1906; asst. dep. min. of pub. wks., 1908.

SALE, GILBERT NOEL, B.Sc. (Forestry, Edin.)—B. 1897; ed. King Alfred's Schl., Wantage, Edinburgh Univ. and Imp. Forestry Inst., Oxford; army serv., 1916-19; lieut., R.A. (S.R.); asst. conserv., forests, Cyprus, Mar., 1922; ag. asst. prin. forest offr., 1924; ag. prin. forest offr., 1926-28; dir., forests, Mauritius, Dec., 1928; conserv., forests, July, 1930; do., Palestine, 1935; do., grade F, 1936.

YRIE, MAJ.-GEN. HON. SIR GRANVILLE DE JNE, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (16), V.D.—B. 1865; ed. King's Schl., Ramatta; mem., state parl't., N.S.W., 3 years, n., house of reps., C. of A., 1911-27; asst. sec. for defence, 1919-22; commanded 3rd ht Horse Regt., N.S.W., for 7 yrs. with rank lieut.-col.; served S. Africa, 1900-01 (severely wounded (Queen's med. 4 clasps); European war, 1914-18; commanded 2nd Light Horse g., A.I.F., Egypt; commanded Australian n., Syria; G.O.C., Australian Forces, Egypt (wounded twice, five times ment. in desps.); maj.-gen. commdg. 1st Cav. Divn., N.S.W., 1923-27; high comsnr. for Australia, 1927-32.

ST. QUINTIN, C.—Clk. to A.A.G., Griqualand W., 1879 border pol., 1880; ag. sub-inspr., Cape pol., 1884; sub-inspr., Bechuanaland pol., 1884; lieut., Bechuanaland border pol., 1885; inspr. of native reserves and J.P., 1887; storekeeper, 1889.

SALMON, FREDERICK JOHN, M.C., A.C.G.I., F.R.G.S., Lieut.-Col., R. of O., R.E.—B. 1882; ed. Univ. Coll. Schl., Lond., and Cent. Tech. Coll., S. Kensington; emplt. on Transvaal Mines, 1904-1907; assoc., inst. of mining and metallurgy; asst. supt., surveys, Ceylon, June, 1908; supt., surveys, 1914; served with field survey units in France, 1915-19; thrice ment. in desps.; lieut.-col., supt., surveys, on staff (appln. surveys), Ceylon, 1924; ag. asst. survr.-gen. 1928; dir., land registrn., and surveys, Cyprus, Sept., 1930; offr. commdg. sp. constab., 1932; dir., surveys, Palestine, 1933.

ST. CLAIR-THOMPSON, GUY WARREN.—B. 1906; ed. Malvern and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A. and dipl., forestry; forest botanist, state research dept., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1927; asst. conserv., forests, Gold Coast, Aug., 1934. ST. JOHNSTON, LIEUT.-COL. SIR REGINALD, C.M.G. (1931), C.M.G. (1923), Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple); M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lond., c.—Ed. Queen Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey and Heltenham Coll.; various appts. in home civ. service; entered Fiji civ. serv., 1907; comsnr. Lau Islands, 1909; seconded for European war; D.A.D.M.S., B.E.F., France; subsequently attached to W.O. (brevet lieut.-col. and recd. ranks of S. of S.); col. sec., Falkland Is., Dec., 1918; ag. gov., Falkland Is., from Sept. 1919 to Apr., 1920; col. sec., Leeward Islands, Dec., 1919; ag. gov., Leeward Is., eight times; admstd. govt. of Antigua on many occasions; ag. admstr., Dominica, 1925; admstr., St. Kitts-Nevis, 9th May, 1925; assumed duty, 23rd Nov., 1925; gov., Leeward Is., Sept., 1929; ret., 1935; author of "The Lau Islands, Fiji," "The Children of the Sun," "The Falkland Islands," "The Islanders of the Pacific," "South Sea Reminiscences," "West Indian Tales" and some novels, etc.
CRIVENOR, T. V.—B. 1908; ed. King's L. Canterbury and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A., ); apptd. temp. asst. prin., C.O., Oct., 1930; .. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1933. CULLIN, Rt. HON.

ST. JULIAN, HERBERT PAUL.—B. 1866; clk., postal dept., Fiji, June, 1883; clk., G.P.O., Aug., 1886; clk. and acctnt., Aug., 1888; ag. col. postmr., Jan., 1905; col. postmr., Sept., 1905; M.L.C., Oct., 1913; J.P., Feb., 1917.
SCOTT W. J.—b. 1922; ed. Queens Pk., Glasgow; Univ. of Glasgow; mil. serv., 1942-46; wat. engr. N. Rhod. 1953; senr. (hydro) 1960-64.

ST. JOHNSTON, THOMAS REGINALD, J.P., F.R.G.S., F.Z.S.—Ed. at Cheltenham Coll.; final bar certif. (Middle Temple) 1914; entd. Fiji civ. ser., 1907; mem. prov. roads board, and D.M.O., Nadroga, 1907; asst. comsnr. for native affairs, comsnr. of sup. ct., and stip. mag., Lau, 1909; dep. comsnr. of stamps, 1910; dep. sheriff, Lau, 1912; dist. comsnr., Lau Is., 1913; on military service, May, 1917; col. sec., Falkland Is., Dec., 1918.
JAMES HENRY, P.C. — to Commonwealth H. of R. for Corangamite, toria, 1910; defeated, gen. el., 1913; M.H.R. Yarra, Victoria, since 1922; prime min. of C'wealth of Australia, 1929-31.

READ, SIR HERBERT JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1918), C.B. (1914), C.M.G. (1907).—B. 1863; M.A., Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 1st class math. mods., and 1st class math. finals; clk., higher div., W.O., Feb., 1887; 2nd class clk., C.O., Jan., 1889; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Sept., 1896; priv. sec. to Mr. (afterwards Sir. E.) Wingfield, Feb., 1898; 1st cls. clk., Oct., 1898; principal clk., 1st Apr., 1906; British delegate at African liquor confce. at Brussels, 1899; one of the Brit. delegates on boundy. comen. at Berlin for the settmt. of Anglo-German frontier in E. Africa, 1906; one of the Brit. plenipotentiaries at African liquor confce. at Brussels, 1906; mem. of hon. man. comtee. of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908; chmn. of deptmtl. comtee. to enquire into W. African med. staff, 1909; visited E. Africa. 1911-12; sen. mem., C.O. delegation at Paris Peace Confce., 1919; asst. under-sec. of state, 10th March, 1916; registr. of the Order of Saint Michael and Saint George; govt., Mauritius, 1st Sept., 1924.

ST. LAURENT, ARTHUR, C.E.—Grad. of Polytechnic schl., Montreal, 1885; b. 1859; apptd. draftsman, Dominion pub. wks., Canada, 1888; asst. engr., Winnipeg residency, for Manitoba and N.W.T., 1890; prin. asst. engr. for Dominion pub. wks., 1897; engr. in charge of Georgian Bay ship canal surveys, 1904; asst. ch. engr., 1906; asst. dep. min. of pub. wks., 1908.
SHIELDS, W. P.—Labour inspection offr., E. Africa Prot., Apl., 1919.

ST. QUINTIN, C.—Clk. to A.A.G., Griqualand W., 1879 border pol., 1880; ag. sub-inspr., Cape pol., 1884; sub-inspr., Bechuanaland pol., 1884; lieut., Bechuanaland border pol., 1885; inspr. of native reserves and J.P., 1887; storekeeper, 1889.

ALDWYN, RT. HON. SIR MICHAEL EDWARD HICKS BEACH, 9th Baronet (created 1619), P.C., M.P.—B. 1837; ed. at Eton and Chr. Ch., Oxon, first class in law and modern history at the final exam., July, 1858; B.A., 1858; M.A., 1861; dep.-lieut. for Gloucestershire, 1861; M.P. for E. Gloucestershire, July, 1864; parly. sec. to the poor law bd., Feb., 1868; under S. of S. for the home dept., Aug. to Dec., 1868; ch. sec. to the lord-lieut. of Ireland, and sworn a mem. of the priv. coun., Feb., 1874; admitted to a seat in the cabinet, 1876; S. of S. for Cols., 4th Feb., 1878, to 28th Apr., 1880; chancellor of the exchequer and leader of the House of Commons, 24th June, 1885, to Feb., 1886; ch. sec. to the lord-lieut. of Ireland, Aug., 1886, to Jan., 1887; pres. of the bd. of trade 1888-92; chancellor of the exchequer, 1895-1902; M.P. for W. Bristol, 1885, to Dec., 1905; visited Straits Settlements as umpire in Tanjong Pagar Dock Arbitration, 1905-6.

SEDGWICK, RICHARD ROMNEY.—B. 1894; l. Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; hol., Trinity Coll., 1912; 1st cls. hist. tripos, . I, 1914 and pt. II, 1915; Gladstone prize, 914; elected fellow of Trinity Coll., 1919; M.A., 919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme asst. rin., Min. of Labour, 1919; asst. prin., C.O., 1922; vte. sec. to parly. under sec. of State, D.O., 3rd Sept., 1929; prin., D.O., 1st Oct., 1930.

EAGER, BASIL WILLIAM.—B. 1898; ed. oad; served R.A., Intell. staff and Ottoman ndarmerie Commn., 1916-22; communications st., F. O., 1926; Br. pro-consul, Jeddah, Dec., 6; detached, sp. duty as ag. polit. offr., Aden ring Anglo-Yemen treaty negotiations, 1933-; resigned F. O. appt. on appt. as frontier offr., en, Sept., 1934; ag. polit. secy., Sept.-Oct., 5 and from May, 1936 to Sept., 1936.
SHIPWAY, WALTER ROBERT.—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd div. clk., and assigned to C.O., Dec., 1898; minor staff clk., 1911.
SHIELD, CHARLES GEOFFRY.—B. 1896; on mily. service (France) 1914-18; on staff, 1918-19; priv. secy. to gov., and clk. to couns., Seychelles, 1920.

ST. JOHNSTON, LIEUT.-COL. THOMAS REGINALD.—Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple); M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lond., etc.; ed. Cheltenham Coll.; served under L.G.B.; entered Fiji civ. serv., 1907; D.M.O. and sec., roads bd., Nadroga Prov., 1908; asst. comsnr. for native affairs, comsnr. of supreme court, and stip. mag. Lau, 1909; dep. comsnr. of stamps, 1910; dep. sheriff. Lau, 1912; comsnr. of Lau Islands, 1913; seconded for European war; lieut. attached contingent of Fijians; capt. and D.A.D.M.S., northern area of France; attached to W.O. as staff-major; col. sec., Falkland Is., Dec., 1918; assumed duty, 15th Sept., 1919; ag. gov., Falkland Is., from 27th Sept., 1919.

SEHEULT, JOSEPH EMMANUEL.—B. 1881; entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, Mar., 1902; ch. clk., dept. of agric., Jan., 1916; asst. censor, 1914; censor, Apr., 1918; man.-sec., agri. bank, Jan., 1928. SEHEULT, LEO GABRIEL, A.C.G.I., A.M. Inst.

ST. AUBYN, GEOFFREY PETER.—Called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Apr., 1880; W. circuit, pol. mag., Kingstown, St. Vincent, 1891; mem. legis. coun., 1891; ag. ch. just., June, 1893; ag. treas., Nov., 1893; ag. atty.-gen., Dec. to May, 1894, and Nov., 1895; ag. ch. just., from Nov., 1896, to July, 1898; judge of Turks and Caicos Islds., July, 1898; ag. comsnr., 1899, 1900, 1901, 1903, 1904, 1905 and 1906; res. mag., Jamaica, 1908.

C.E.—B. 1886; asst. dist. offr., pub. works, Trinidad, Mar., 1911; 2nd grade dist. offr., Sept., 1916; 1st grade dist. offr., June, 1917; gen. asst. to asst. D.P.W., Jan., 1925; exec. engnr., July, 1930; divnl. engnr., Jan., 1936.
SIKES, H.L.—Govt. hydraulic engnr., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., Jan., 1919.
SILVESTER, J. M.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913.

SEEL, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1895; ed. Macclesfield Grammar Schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (schol.); on mily. serv. Cheshire Regt. in Gallipoli, Egypt and Palestine; apptd. to air miny., 28th July, 1919; seconded for serv. in C.O., as asst. prin., 9th Jan., 1922; pvte. sec. to parly. under secy. of state for the Cols. (Mr. Wm. Lunn, M.P.), 10th June 1929; prin., 17th Sept., 1929.
SIM, HON. WM. ALEXANDER.—B. 1858; judge of sup. ct. of New Zealand, 16th Jan., 1911.

SEALY J. ERNEST CAMERON. — 2nd clk., audit and registry. dept., Tobago, Feb., 1888; clk. to comsurs. and financial bd., Nov., 1890, and Oct., 1893; sec. to bd. of educn., Sept., 1892; ch. clk. treasury, June, 1893; postmr. of Tobago, Dec. 1895.

ST. AUBYN, GEOFFREY PETER.—Called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Apr., 1880; W. circuit, pol. mag., Kingstown, St. Vincent, 1891; mem. legis. coun., 1891; ag. ch. just., June, 1893; ag. treas., Nov., 1893; ag. atty.-gen., Dec. to May, 1894, and Nov., 1895; ag. ch. just., from Nov., 1896, to July, 1898; judge of Turks and Caicos Islds., July, 1898; ag. comsnr., 1899, 1900, 1901, 1903, 1904, 1905 and 1906; res. mag., Jamaica, 1908.
SILBERRAD, HUBERT.—Ed. at Wren's and Finishing Tech. Coll. (engineering); asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 8th May, 1903; transfd. to Nyasaland Prot. as 2nd grade res., July, 1909.

SHIRCORE, JOHN OWEN, M.B., Ch.B.—Med. offr., Nyasaland, 1908; transfd. to Uganda, 1909; re-transfd. as med. offr., Nyasaland, 1910; med. offr., E.A.P., 1912; dep. prin. med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 15th Oct., 1919.

SHORT, WILLIAM STONHAM, I.S.O. (1919).—B. 1852; solr.; ed. gram. schl., Walthamstow, Foundation Schl., London, and Camden Coll., Sydney; clk., P.W.D., N. Zealand, 1874; ch. clk., lands dept., 1898; asst. under-sec., pub. wks., 1909; under-sec., 1916. SHORTT, ADAM, C.M.G. (1911); M.A., F.R.S.C.—B. 1859; ed. at Walkerton High Schl. and Queen's Univ., Canada (B.A., 1883, M.A., 1885) and Glasgow and Edin. Univs.; asst. prof. of philosophy, Queen's Univ., 1885; lect. and prof. of polit. science, 1889-1908; civ. ser. comsnr., Canada, 1908-1917; chmn. of historical manuscripts publication board, Oct., 1917; writer on history, banking and economics.
SIMSON, COLIN COAPE.—Chief mcd. offr., Papua, 19th Oct., 1908.
SIMSON, H. B.—Temp. asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1919. SIMSON, J. A.—Ed. in England, Germany and Switzerland; agt.-gen. in India for the British Protectorates in Africa.
SIERRA LEONE, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. JOHN WALMSLEY, D.D.—B. 1867; formerly vice-principal of Wycliff Hall, Oxford, vicar of St. Ann's, Nottingham, and hon. canon of Southwell; consecrated, 1910.
SIMPSON, GEORGE.—Inspr. of schls., Bermuda, 1880; also inspr. of dockyard schls.

SIMMONS, HON. WM. CHAS.—B. 1865; ed. at Collingwood and Owen Sound High Schls.; B.A., Toronto Univ., 1895; called to the bar, N.W.T., 1900; elec. to legis. assem. of Alberta for Lethbridge dist., 1906; resig., 1908; puisne judge of sup. ct., Alberta. 12th Oct., 1910.

SHUFFREY, PAUL.—Ed. St. Paul's and Oxford; open schlr. of Lincoln, 1908; B.A. (greats), 1912; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Leone, 1913; priv. sec. to gov., 1914-15; ag. dist. comsnr., Imperri, 1915 and 1916-17; ag. priv. sec. to gov., 1918.

SIMPSON, SAMUEL.—B. 1876; ed. at Owen's Coll., and Edin. Univ.; B.Sc.; Stevens scholar; sec. to the Union; sen. pres. of students' representative coun.; Highland and agric. socy.'s prizeman; life mem. and silver medallist of roy. agric. socy.; holder of nat. diploma in agric.; sen. lecturer in agric. to govt. agric. coll., Ghizeh, Egypt; cotton expert to B.C.A. Prot., Mar., 1905; dir. of agric., Uganda, 1912; author of "Report on Cotton Growing Industry in B.C.A. Prot."; reported on the agric. resources of Angola, Trinidad, Tobago, B. Guiana and Surinam. SIMS, JAMES HUGH.—B. 1878; sub.-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1905; inspr., 1913 asst. comsr., 1918.

SEAGOE, ERNEST GOLDFINCH, M.B.E.—B. 36; clk. to res. comsnr., New Hebrides, 1902; spr. of native labourers, Br. residency, New brides, 1909; ag. asst. to res. comsnr., 1911-12 d 1915-16; joint ag. native advocate, Oct., 15, to Dec., 1922; ag. asst. to res., Nov., 1919 Feb., 1920 and Sept. to Dec., 1921; commdt., nstab., Aug., 1922; dep. comsnr., W. Pacific ew Hebrides). Nov., 1922; in ch. of accts., ay to Aug., 1923; ag. asst. to res. comsnr.. various occasions, 1923-33; dist. agt. and lab. spr., Apr., 1923; agt., Efate dist. and commdt., l. (Br. divn.) Apr., 1925.

SIMSON, ERIC ANDREW.—B. 1895; ed. Wellington Coll. (schlr., 1909), and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; on mily. serv., R.F.A., 17th div., Nov., 1914 to Feb., 1916; staff, 12th div., Feb. to Dec., 1916; capt., R.A.F., Jan., 1917 to June, 1919; apptd. under re-construction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 3rd Aug., 1920.
SYMONDS C. H.—Commissioner of audit, Victoria, 13th Oct. 1857.
STRACHEY WILLIAM.—Appointed by Earl Grey in 1848 to be précis writer to the colonial office; retired 1870; was formerly in the Bengal civil service.

SEED, CHARLES WILLIAM STANLEY.—B. 1898; d. St. John's (R.C.) Coll., Wouersh, Surrey; artists' Rifles, 1916; 2nd lieut., 12th London Regt., 1917; served in France, 1918-19; (G.S. nd Victory Meds.); col. audit dept., July, 1921; asst. audr., Nigeria, Sept., 1921; ag. senr. asst. udr., July-Sept., 192; asst. audr., Nyasaland, Dec., 1926; ag. senr. asst. audr., June, 1927 to July, 1928 and Oct., 1928 to Sept., 1929; audr., Seychelles and Somaliland, July, 1930; also pvte. sec. to gov., Seychelles, 1931-33 and to O.A.G., July-Sept., 1933; senr. asst. audr., S.S. and F.M.S., 1936.

SHUCKBURGH, JOHN EVELYN, C.B. (1918). —B. 1877; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1906; apptd. jnr. clk., store dept., I. O., Oct., 1900; transfd. to judl. and pub. dept., Mar., 1901; joint editor, "India Office List," Oct., 1901; priv. sec. to permnt. under-sec. of state, July, 1902; snr. clk., polit. dept., Mar., 1906; editor, telegraphic codes, Aug., 1907 to Nov., 1912; asst. sec., polit. dept., Oct., 1912; sec., polit. dept., Mar., 1917; ag. asst. under sec. of state, C. O., 1st Mar., 1921.
C E.—B. 1868; ed. secondary and private schls.; joined lands and survey dept., N.Z., 1891; under-sec. for lands, 1922.

SOMERS (6TH BARON, CR. 1784), ARTHUR HERBERT SOMERS COCKS, K.C.M.G. (1926), D.S.O. (1919), M.C. (1917).—B. 1887; ed. Charterhouse and New Coll., Oxford; serv. in Great War, 1914-19; capt., 1st Life Guards, 1914; lieut.-col., Tank Corps, 1918-19; ret., 1922; a lord in waiting in ordinary to the King, 1924; govr. of Victoria, 1926-31.

FREDERIC BARKER, D.D.—Educated at Grantham School and at Jesus College, Cambridge, where he graduated A.B. 1829, M.A. 1835, D.D. 1854; became incumbent of Upton, Cheshire, 1830; of St. Mary's, Edge Hill, Liverpool, 1835; and of Baslow, Derbyshire, 1854; consecrated 1854; is author of "A Commentary on Twenty-six Psalms," &c.; is metropolitan bishop in Australia. The diocese originally included the whole of Australia, and bore that name; but in 1847 it was restricted to the central portion of the colony, and called by its present title, while additional bishops were nominated for Newcastle, Adelaide, Melbourne, and Perth; by patent, 19th October, 1854. The bishop is a metropolitan bishop, whose suffragans are the bishops of the above dioceses.

SIMMONS, WILLIAM CHARLES.—B. 1865; ed. Cheltenham Coll., pupil and asst. to Messrs. Ransomes & Rapier, Ipswich, 1883-1889; clk. of wks., Badulla hosp., Ceylon, 1890; dist. engnr., Ceylon, 1891; asst. comsnr. of wks., Fiji, 1899; ag. comsnr. of wks., 1909; dep. comsnr. of wks., 1911; ag. comsnr. of wks., and tempy. M.L.C., Feb., 1916; ag. comsnr. of wks., Oct., 1918; tempy. M.L.C., Oct., 1918.

SIFTON, HON. SIR CLIFFORD, K.C.M.G. (1915), K.C.—B. 1861, Co. Middlesex, Ont.; ed. London, Ont., High Schl. and Victoria Univ. (Prince of Wales Medallist, 1880); called to the bar of Manitoba, 1882; elec. to legis. assem., Man., 1888; atty.-gen. and min. of educn., 1891-96; codified laws relating to civil procedure in Man.; had charge of negotiations with federal govt. respecting the Manitoba Schl. Law; Q.C. (Dominion), 1895; called to federal cabinet as min. of the interior, 1896; elec. to H. of C. by accl. for Brandon; British agt. before the Alaska Bndry. Tribunal, 1903; re-elect. to H. of C. 1900 and 1904; resig. from the cabinet, 1905; one of Canadian representatives at internat. confce. at Washington on preservation of natural resources, 1908; re-elect. to H. of C., 1908; chmn., Canadian comsn. of conservation 1909-1918; did not seek re-elect. at g.e., 1911.

S POWERS, JAMES HOWARD.—B. 1889; authorised survr., Queensland, Oct., 1911; survr., F.M.S., Nov., 1913; asst. supt., surveys, Jan., 1919; asst. supt., trig. surveys, F.M.S., Nov., 1922; asst. supt., rev. surveys, Sept., 1924; ag. supt., surveys, Johore, Jan. 1931; do., Kedah, Oct., 1932; supt., surveys, F.M.S., and S.S., June, 1933.

SYDNEY (AUSTRALIA), BISHOP of, and METROPOLITAN.—Bishopric founded 1847.—Right Rev. Alfred Barry, D.D., D.C.L.; educated at King's College, London, and Trinity College, Cambridge: B.A. (4th wrangler, 2nd Smith's prizeman, 7th in, 1st class classical tripos, 1880); fellow of Trinity, 1849; head master of Leeds Grammar School 1854-62; principal of Cheltenham College, 1862-68; principal of King's College, London, 1868-83; when he was consecrated to this see; was canon residentiary of Worcester, 1871 to 1881; canon of Westminster, 1881-83; hon. chaplain to the Queen, 1879-83.

THOMPSON, HERBERT W.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., 4th Oct., 1915; on mil. ser. from 13th Aug., 1917 to 9th Apr., 1919; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., 18th June, 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; promoted to 2nd div. clk., 24th Feb., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925. THOMPSON, JOHN BAIRD, C.B.E. (1929), M.N.Z. Soc.

THOMPSTONE, CAPT. ERIC WESTBURY, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Shrewsbury Schl.; 2nd lieut., King's Shropshire Light Infy., 1915; served in France and Italy, 1916-19; wounded; silver med. for valour (Italy); adminis. offr., N.P., Nigeria, July, 1919.

THOMPSON, JOHN HARRISON.—B. 1892; matric., London Univ., 1912; diploma, English language and lit., London Univ., 1921; appt. after compet. exam., asst. clk., commercial intelligence branch, B. of T., 23rd June, 1912; library attendant, C.O., 6th Apr., 1914; after further compet. exam., apptd. as clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to C.O., 24th Aug., 1914; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; asst. librarian, 10th Oct., 1925; ag. regisar., col. laws and asst. librarian, 19th Nov., 1928.

M.I.C.E.—B. 1857; asst. engnr. govt. rlys., Trinidad, 1874; dist. engnr., Couva extension rly., 1878; res. engnr. in charge of construction, San Fernando and Guaiacara rlys., 1879; 1st asst. engnr., P.W. dept., Jan., 1885; has acted as asst. dir. of P.W., 1885, 6, 8, 9, and 90; engnr., N. div., P.W.D., Jan., 1892; 1st engnr. in charge of rds. and bdges., P.W.D., Jan., 1894; engnr. in charge of rds. and bdges, rds. and bdges. dept., Jan., 1895; asst. dir. of pub. wks., and sen. div. engnr., Jan., 1897; has acted as D.P.W. with seat in legis. coun., 1894, 5, 8, 9, 1900, 2, 5, 6, and 7.

THOMSON, REGINALD FRANCOIS, F.R.A.I., F.R.G.S.—B. 1879; ch. inspr., native labourers for Br. Solomon Is. Prot., Dec., 1920; ag. temp. dist. offr., Shortland, and dep. comsnr. for W. Pacific in Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1925-27. THOMSON, WALTER MORRIS.—B. 1905; ed. Edinburgh Univ.; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., Dec., 1928.
TOH CHIONG HIENG.—b. 1910 ; ed. Anglo-Chinese Sch., Seremban and King Edward VII Coll. of Medicine, S'pore ; M.O., S'pore, 1938.

THORNE, RICHARD ALFRED.—B. 1902; apptd after compet. exam., asst. clk., sec.'s office, G.P.O., 24th Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transfd. to C.O., 15th Oct., 1920. THORNTON, LIEUT.-COL. (TEMP. COL.), SIR EDWARD NEWBURY, K.B.E. (1919), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. Lond.), D.P.H. (Camb.), S.A.M.C., Esq. of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem (1924).—B. 1878; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and London Hosp.; asst. med. offr., Imp. Yeomanry Hospitals, Anglo-Boer war, 1900-01; plague serv., Punjab, 1902-03; ent. civ. serv., Cape Colony, 1903; additional M.O.H., Cape Colony, 1906; med. inspr. of hosps. and charitable insts., and ch. local govt. inspr., Cape, 1910; major, med. br., sec. 1, Union Res. of Offrs. in German S.W. Africa, 1915; (ment. in despa.); Lieut.-Col., S.A.M.C. and O.C. S.
